title: transfer — model relationships
description: Four owned models with a hub-and-spoke shape: XferJob is the aggregate root; XferShard, XferArtifact, XferMappingProfile hang off it. Every model belongs to a tenant. No other module holds an FK to transfer tables — inbound integration happens via #[Importable] / #[Exportable] attribute-driven registration + HasActivityLog / HasAudit dual-write on transitions.

internal.description: Shards for sharded imports / exports and multi-sheet workbooks. Cascade-delete with the parent job.
The uploaded file for imports. Purged before the row (see retention.json).
The export result file or import success artifact.
Mapping profile applied at request time, if any. `XferMappingProfile` uses soft-delete so a historical job keeps a readable reference until final hard-delete.
Nullable — templates and pre-request artifacts are not tied to a job.

outbound.description: Nullable when a platform-admin runs a system-level operation (rare). Cascade so tenant deletion removes the audit trail too.
Polymorphic initiator. Typically `Stackra\User\Models\User`, may be `Stackra\Auth\Models\ServiceAccount` for API-driven jobs.
Polymorphic canceller. Null when the job succeeded / failed on its own.
Denormalised from parent for scan predicates.
Polymorphic causer of the artifact write. Typically User; may be System for scheduled jobs producing templates.

Profile ownership. Cascade so removing a user drops their private profiles. Shared profiles (`is_shared=true`) transfer ownership to a tenant admin before the delete (handled by user::UserErased listener).

inbound.description: No other module holds an FK to transfer tables. Inbound integration is attribute-driven: any model in any module carrying #[Importable] / #[Exportable] / #[SampleData] is discovered by the compiler at boot and registered in EntityRegistry. HasActivityLog on XferJob writes a row into activity_log (activity module) on every status transition; HasAudit on XferJob writes a row into audits (audit module) on the same signal — both are morphMany from the perspective of activity + audit, not FKs on our side.
